_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Glaucoma and Melanoma of the iris
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Closed-angle glaucoma- primary or secondary to prior ocular surgery (peripheral iridectomy)
Melanoma of the iris (Malignant tumor of the iris)
Prior to cataract surgery in some cases (preparatory iridectomy)

preauth.label: Clinical Photograph of the affected eye

claim.label: Histopathology report (incase of Melanoma of iris)
Intraoperative photograph with patient ID, time and date (Optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Did the clinical examination suggest presence of Closed angle Glaucoma/ Melanoma as the reason for conducting Iridectomy?
If the clinical examination did not suggest presence of Closed angle Glaucoma/ Melanoma, were other reasons for conducting Iridectomy specified?
Detailed Clinical notes including clinical symptoms, indications (such as closed angle Glaucoma/ Melanoma of iris, etc.) and examination/ investigations (Tonometry/ Gonisocopy)?
Clinical photo of Affected Eye whether R or L with full face photograph?

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and outcomes of the surgery?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?

common_queries: Clinical examination did not suggest presence of Closed angle Glaucoma/ Melanoma as the reason for conducting Iridectomy, and no other reasons were specified.
Iridectomy performed without documented clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination, or prior conservative medical therapy failure.
Clinical photograph of the affected eye is missing R/L labelling or a full face photograph.
Mandatory Tonometry report or Gonioscopy report missing at pre-authorization.
Operative notes do not detail the steps of surgery performed or outcomes.
Histopathology report missing despite Melanoma of iris being indicated.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Availability of Ophthalmic Laser equipment (if laser iridectomy is being performed)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Iridectomy only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination and does not respond to conservative medical therapy.
Iridectomy is a procedure involving removal of a small, full-thickness piece of the iris. In recent years, lasers have also been used to perform iridectomies.
